title: 3Sum
description:
Given an integer array `nums`, return all the triplets `[nums[i], nums[j],
nums[k]]` such that `i`, `j`, `k` are distinct and `nums[i] + nums[j] +
nums[k] == 0`. The solution set must not contain duplicate triplets.

Return the triplets sorted (each triplet ascending, and the list of triplets in
ascending order).

solutions.summary: Fix one number, then two-pointer scan the rest for its complement pair.

steps.title: Sort and set up the result
steps.explanation: Sorting lets us skip duplicates and move two pointers by comparing the running sum to zero.

code.python:
def three_sum(nums):
    nums.sort()
    res = []
    return res

steps.title: Fix the first number
steps.explanation: Loop `i` over each value as the first of the triplet, skipping repeats so triplets stay unique.

steps.title: Two-pointer the remainder
steps.explanation: Move `lo`/`hi` inward: grow the sum by raising `lo`, shrink it by lowering `hi`, and record every zero, skipping duplicates.
